schools.summary: Gill: these were the kings on the west side of Jordan, distinct from Sihon and Og already conquered; their heart melted at the drying of Jordan.

themes: their heart melted, neither was there spirit in them any more
God breaking the courage of the enemy
no courage remained in them (Josh. 2:11)

schools.summary: Gill: after they had crossed Jordan and pitched at Gilgal, when the dread had seized Canaan — a fit time for the command to circumcise again.

themes: Make thee sharp knives, and circumcise again the children of Israel the second time
the covenant-sign renewed before the conquest
circumcision the token of the covenant (Gen. 17:11)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua did not circumcise them himself but ordered and saw it done; circumcision was not restricted to one sort of person; done at the hill of the foreskins.

themes: Joshua made him sharp knives, and circumcised the children of Israel at the hill of the foreskins
prompt obedience setting right the neglect
the circumcision made without hands (Col. 2:11)

schools.summary: Gill: the reason of the command, namely what follows.
Keil & Delitzsch: all the males that came out of Egypt having died in the wilderness.

themes: All the people that came out of Egypt, that were males... died in the wilderness
the covenant carried on to the children
their carcases fell in the wilderness (Heb. 3:17)

schools.summary: Gill: all that came out of Egypt, males, were circumcised; the wilderness-born had not been.

themes: all the people that were born in the wilderness... them they had not circumcised
the covenant-sign suspended in the years of judgment
your children shall wander in the wilderness (Num. 14:33)

schools.summary: Gill: the forty years, wanting a few days, the round number given.
Ellicott: the first person 'us' used, knitting the wilderness story to the entrance.

themes: the children of Israel walked forty years in the wilderness... unto whom the LORD sware that he would not shew them the land
the long judgment on the unbelieving generation
they should not enter into my rest (Ps. 95:11)

schools.summary: Gill: those born in the wilderness, who succeeded the fathers, some near forty; those born before had been circumcised, but not these, not circumcised by the way.

themes: their children, whom he raised up in their stead, them Joshua circumcised
the new generation sealed with the covenant
the promise unto you, and to your children (Acts 2:39)

schools.summary: Gill: it seems done in one day; Bishop Usher thinks the day after, on the eleventh of Nisan; they abode till whole.

themes: they abode in their places in the camp, till they were whole
faith resting defenceless under God's keeping
he that keepeth thee will not slumber (Ps. 121:3)

schools.summary: Gill: the LORD said this out of the tabernacle.
Keil & Delitzsch: the reproach of Egypt rolled away when the circumcision was completed, the place named Gilgal.

themes: This day have I rolled away the reproach of Egypt from off you... the name of the place is called Gilgal
God removing the reproach of his people
God hath taken away my reproach (Gen. 30:23)

schools.summary: Gill: they encamped in Gilgal and kept the passover there, little more than a mile from Jericho.
Keil & Delitzsch: kept the passover on the fourteenth at even in the plains of Jericho.

themes: kept the passover on the fourteenth day of the month at even in the plains of Jericho
the feast of redemption at the gate of the land
Christ our passover; let us keep the feast (1 Cor. 5:7-8)

schools.summary: Gill: the old corn of the last year, which they might eat, the new not lawful until the wave sheaf was offered; unleavened cakes and parched corn that same day.

themes: they did eat of the old corn of the land on the morrow after the passover
God's provision passing to the fruit of the land
the manna giving way to the corn of Canaan

schools.summary: Gill: there was now no further need of it; miracles are not continued when unnecessary; the manna, a type of Christ, ceased.

themes: the manna ceased on the morrow after they had eaten of the old corn of the land
the extraordinary giving way to the ordinary
the manna a type of Christ, the true bread from heaven (John 6:32)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua was on the border of Jericho, reconnoitring alone, seeking where to make his first attack; he saw a man with sword drawn and asked, Art thou for us, or for our adversaries?

themes: there stood a man over against him with his sword drawn... Art thou for us, or for our adversaries?
a majestic figure at the threshold of war
a greater Captain met by the captain of the conquest

schools.summary: Gill: he was not come for or on the side of their adversaries, as Joshua suspected.
Keil & Delitzsch: as captain of the host of the LORD, now come.

themes: as captain of the host of the LORD am I now come. And Joshua fell on his face... and did worship
the Captain a divine person, receiving worship
Christ the captain of their salvation (Heb. 2:10)

schools.summary: Gill: this was a trial and proof of Joshua's obedience.
Clarke: the same words the angel spoke to Moses at Sinai (Exod. 3:5), so it seems likely it was the same Person.

themes: Loose thy shoe from off thy foot; for the place whereon thou standest is holy
the same word as at the burning bush
Christ present making holy ground (Exod. 3:5)
